root_cause_summary: The User model defines an 'email' attribute, but repository.py line 66 incorrectly accesses 'email_address', causing AttributeError.

candidate_targets.rationale: Contains the exact line from traceback (line 66) with the incorrect attribute access 'user.email_address'. The excerpt shows a comment indicating this is a bug.

repair_plan: Remove or update the '# type: ignore[attr-defined]' comment since the attribute will now be correct
validation_focus: Syntax: Ensure no syntax errors after attribute name change
Type checking: Verify mypy/pyright no longer flags attribute access
Unit tests: Run tests/test_repository.py::TestRepositoryEmailDomain to verify email domain extraction works
additional_notes: The User model in models.py confirms the attribute is 'email', not 'email_address'
The bug is isolated to a single line; no other files need modification
Tests indicate the expected domain for user id=1 is 'example.com'
